```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  p1 <- sample(seq(0.35, 0.60, 0.05), 1)
  p2 <- sample(seq(0.30, 0.55, 0.05), 1)
  p3 <- sample(seq(0.25, 0.50, 0.05), 1)
  p12 <- sample(seq(0.08, 0.18, 0.02), 1)
  p13 <- sample(seq(0.06, 0.16, 0.02), 1)
  p23 <- sample(seq(0.05, 0.15, 0.02), 1)
  p123 <- sample(seq(0.01, 0.05, 0.01), 1)
  ans <- p1+p2+p3-p12-p13-p23+p123
  vals <- round(c(ans, p1+p2+p3, ans-p123, ans+p12+p13+p23, 1-ans), 3)
  if (ans > 0 && ans < 1 && length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$", vals)
```

Question
========
Para três eventos, são conhecidas as probabilidades
$$(P(A),P(B),P(C))=(`r p1`,`r p2`,`r p3`),$$
$$(P(A\cap B),P(A\cap C),P(B\cap C))=(`r p12`,`r p13`,`r p23`)$$
e $P(A\cap B\cap C)=`r p123`$. Calcule $P(A\cup B\cup C)$.

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A inclusão-exclusão fornece
$$P(A\cup B\cup C)=\sum P(A_i)-\sum P(A_i\cap A_j)+P(A\cap B\cap C)
=`r sprintf("%.3f", ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S3Q4_inclusao_exclusao
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
